observations.observation: Zero new CBSAs this snapshot — geoDiffusionRate dropped to 0% with 14 active metros reported by quant metrics (down from 23 last heartbeat). This is likely a window artifact in the wildfire metric calculation rather than actual contraction; cumulative snapshot data showed 23 active CBSAs last cycle. The burst-quiet pattern continues: after 6 new CBSAs last heartbeat, the network went silent this cycle.

Token velocity held at 0.57x with acceleration at 1.006x — essentially flat. The 7d trailing window ticked up from 435 to 445 receipts ($24,729→$24,993), a modest +10 receipt gain. After the sharp deceleration two heartbeats ago (0.531x), velocity has stabilized in the 0.55-0.57x band without meaningful recovery or further decline.

AAA appeared in the top brand list this snapshot — a roadside assistance / insurance / travel services brand, distinct from the grocery, home improvement, and QSR brands that have dominated the list. This is the first service-sector brand to enter the top list, suggesting spend category diversity is genuinely expanding.

Token velocity at 0.566x with acceleration 1.03x — mild recovery continuing after the sharp 0.531x deceleration two heartbeats ago. The 7d trailing window is nearly flat (434→435 receipts), suggesting the rolling-window artifact has cleared but intake hasn't meaningfully accelerated. Geographic expansion and velocity are decoupled this cycle.

6 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (11700, 38060, non-metro CA, 16860, non-metro ID, 31080), pushing total active metros to 23 — the fourth consecutive burst phase after the 31-CBSA peak. The network is expanding in discrete pulses, not linear growth, and this burst includes non-metro California and Idaho for the first time.

A new brand labeled "Store" appeared in the top brand list this snapshot — an ambiguous merchant name that likely represents a store-brand or unresolved merchant. This is the second consecutive heartbeat with a new brand entry (Starbucks appeared last cycle), suggesting the merchant diversity is expanding but resolution quality is inconsistent.

Token velocity recovered slightly to 0.55x with acceleration at 1.037x — a mild positive after the sharp 0.531x deceleration last heartbeat. The 7d trailing window ticked up from $24,539 to $24,716 (425→434 receipts), suggesting the rolling-window artifact that caused the sharp drop has partially cleared. Recovery is modest, not a reversal.

3 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (non-metro MA, CBSA 29420, non-metro AZ), bringing total active metros to 17. The burst-quiet-burst pattern continues: after the large 31-CBSA peak, the network has been expanding in smaller increments (3 new this cycle), with geographic diffusion rate at 17.6% of active CBSAs.

All 5 open vendor requests on the frontier are SaaS/infrastructure vendors (Notion, Render, Vercel, DigitalOcean, Hetzner) — a distinct category from the current physical retail dataset. If approved, these would represent the first digital-native spend tokens in the pipeline, potentially expanding the cross-domain signal beyond its current 0.7% ratio.

Starbucks appeared in the top brand list for the first time this snapshot, joining King Soopers and other established brands. This is a new category signal — a national QSR/coffee chain entering the verified spend dataset alongside grocery and home improvement brands.
